title: Review a JS<->WASM interop change
description: Check type marshalling, memory ownership, and view lifetimes before accepting a change to the boundary.
body: Steps: 1. List the functions and data crossing the boundary in the change. 2. For each non-numeric value, confirm it goes through generated bindings or has a correct, tested manual marshalling (pointer+length+encoding). 3. Verify the ownership contract: who allocates and who frees each linear-memory buffer, freed exactly once. 4. Check that no JS view of memory is cached across a call that can grow the heap. 5. Confirm imports/exports still line up with the loader's import object. Rollback point: the change is reversible per file. Validation: a round-trip test passes for each crossing value and a leak test shows bounded linear memory.
